LAW AND MOTION 302, HEARING RE: DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PUBLIC UTILITIES COMMISSION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. MATTER CONTESTED, ARGUMENT HELD. TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON THE HARASSMENT CLAIM IS DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILED TO MAINTAIN ITS BURDEN OF PRODUCTION. (CCP 437(P)(2).) THE COURT WILL REVIEW THE PROPOSED ORDER SUBMITTED BY MS. SOLOMON. JUDGE: ROCHELLE C. EAST. CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion
Off Calendar Aug-12-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Mathew Schoech, Esq. of Schoech Law Group PC, Tel. 916.569.31940, appearing via Zoom for Plaintiff Lucian Filler;
Suzanne Solomon, Esq. of Liebert Cassidey Whitmore, Tel. 415.512.3000, appearing in person for Defendant California Public Utility Commission.

Hearing re: DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PUBLIC UTILITIES COMMISSION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

The matter is contested. Argument is held.

At the conclusion of argument, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Defendant's motion for summary adjudication on the harassment claim is denied. Defendant failed to maintain its burden of production. (CCP 437(p)(2).)

(See Word minutes for the Court' complete ruling.)

The Court will review the proposed order submitted by Ms. Solomon.

Judge: Rochelle C. East. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Court Reporter: Jane Hong, CSR #11975, Tel. 909.831.2644, Email: csrjane@hotmail.com Bailiff: Sr. Deputy Malone. (302/RCE)

PLAINTIFF LUCIAN FILLER'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO INTERROGATORIES AND REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS (PART 2 OF 2, TEANTAAIVE RULING ONLY)

Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Interrogatories And Requests For Production Of Documents And To Compel Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Nov-09-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF LUCIAN FILLER's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Interrogatories And Requests For Production Of Documents And To Compel Production Of Documents

PLAINTIFF LUCIAN FULLER'S (HEREAFTER EITHER "PLAINTIFF" OR"FULLER") MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORY NO. 201.1(d) AND REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS NOS 14, 19 - 35 & 43 IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART AS FOLLOWS:

(1) PLAINTIFF'S MOTION AS TO FORM INTERROGATORY NO. 201.1(d) IS DENIED, DEFENDANT' AMENDED RESPONSE RENDERS THE MOTION MOOT.

(2) PLAINTIFF'S MOTION AS TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION NO. 14 IS DENIED. DEFENDANT'S SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSE SATISFIES THE REQUIREMENTS OF THE CODE.

(3) PLAINTIFF'S MOTION AS TO DEFENDANT'S RESPONSE TO ITS DOCUMENT PRODUCTION REQUESTS IS DENIED. DEFENDANTS PRODUCTION LOG SATIFIES THE REQUIREMENTS OF THE CODE.

(4) DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS NUMBERED NOS 19 - 30, 32 -34 & 43 ARE OVERRULED WITH THE EXCEPTION OF A CLAIM OF A/C PRIVILEGE.

(5) WITHIN TWENTY (20) CALENDAR DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER, DEFENDANT WILL MAKE FURTHER RESPONSES TO THE REQUESTS IDENTIFIED IN PARAGRAPH (4) ABOVE AND DELIVER TO PLAINTIFF ALL DOCUMENTS RESPONSIVE TO SUCH REQUESTS. TO THE EXTENT THAT DEFENDANT BELIEVES IT HAS PREVIOUSLY PROVIDED PLAINTIFF WITH COPIES IT WILL IDENTIFY IN ITS RESPONSE THE SPECIFIC DOCUMENT PREVIOUSLY PRODUCED. TO THE EXTENT THAT IT IS ASSERTING AN A/C PRIVILEGE AS TO ANY DOCUMENT, IT WILL IDENTIFY THE DOCUMENT WITH SUFFICIENT SPECIFICTY TO ALLOW PLAINTIFF TO CHALLENGE THE CLAIM

(6) PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS NOS 31 AND 35 ARE DENIED. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO REQUESTS ARE SUSTAINED.

(7) THE COURT WILL IMPOSE NO MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST ANY PARTY.

COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF IS TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER CONSISTENT WITH THE COURT'S RULING, APPROVED AS TO FORM BY DEFENDANT (CRC.3.1312) AND EMAILED DIRECTLY TO JUDGE PRO TEM RENNE AT rennepar85@gmail.com IN WORD FORMAT.

Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne, Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JPT)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-06-2022 Continued To Feb-08-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-14-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PUBLIC UTILITIES COMMISSION'S DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

Defendant's "demurrer to first amended complaint" (FAC) is overruled.

The demurrer is based solely on defendant's view that the FAC fails to plead "a cause of action for discrimination [and/or harassment] based on sexual orientation under the Fair Employment and Housing Act." (Dmr. Not. 3:4-11.) I disagree. While this is in the main a whistleblower case, the FAC also adequately pleads the elements of FEHA discrimination and harassment claims. (See, e.g., CACI 2500, 2522A; FAC 2:4-8, 2:26-3:4, 3:9-25, 8:22-12:27, 15:10-16:22.) In modern practice, demurrers are of diminished importance; "wherever possible, cases should be resolved on their merits rather than through technical challenges to the pleadings." (Edmon & Karnow, California Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (TRG 2016) 7:1.)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
